commit.message:
feat: smart registry install - workflow clones to cwd, component installs to components dir

- kdeps registry install <workflow|agency>: extracts to current dir (like git clone),
  prints next steps (cd, cp .env.example, kdeps run)
- kdeps registry install <component>: installs to ./components/ if inside a kdeps
  project, otherwise to ~/.kdeps/components/ (or KDEPS_COMPONENT_DIR)
- peekManifest reads kdeps.pkg.yaml from archive to determine type without full extract
- isKdepsProjectDir detects if cwd contains workflow.yaml/agency.yaml
- ParseKdepsPkgFromBytes added to domain package for in-memory YAML parsing
- Removed --output flag (no longer needed; install dir is determined by type)
- 9 unit tests covering all install paths and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(bench): disable Maglev execution to prevent SIGSEGV in 4 benchmarks

Maglev codegen always deopts at the start due to CreateMappedArguments
falling to the catch-all handler. For simple benchmarks (fib, arithmetic,
property_access), the deopt works correctly. For complex programs
(closures, constructors, deep chains, keyed stores), the deopt triggers
SIGSEGV. Since the result is always a deopt back to the interpreter,
disabling execution has zero performance impact.

This unblocks all 9 precompiled benchmarks so we get complete numbers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: replace GrocyClient with StorageClient for Storage API

- Created StorageClient class calling HA-Storage REST API
- Replaced all GrocyClient usage in main.py and ingress_server.py
- Updated field names: parent_product_id→parent_id, qu_id_stock→unit_id, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use 256MB demand-paged pool for l_img.h

Replaces the 64MB fixed pool with 256MB virtual reservation.
Pages are demand-paged (MAP_ANONYMOUS) — only physical memory for
pages actually touched is committed. Handles images up to ~8000x8000.

The grow-on-demand approach was reverted because stb_image holds
raw pointers into the pool that can't be relocated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: release v0.5.0 — SBE spec compliance improvements (#110)

Bump version to 0.5.0 and update CHANGELOG with all 11 spec compliance
features implemented via TDD:

- #88: <ref> in composites
- #89: Nested composites
- #90: Nested groups
- #91: <data> in groups
- #92: Custom headerType
- #93: Explicit blockLength
- #94: sinceVersion/deprecated on enum values
- #95: sinceVersion on data elements
- #96: characterEncoding attribute
- #97: VarString16/VarString32
- #98: Set choice validation

130 unit tests, 113/115 integration tests (2 pre-existing failures).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add run.py launcher to fix Windows asyncio subprocess NotImplementedError (#12)

On Windows, uvicorn --reload spawns the app in a child process with a
SelectorEventLoop, which raises NotImplementedError for
asyncio.create_subprocess_exec. This breaks all ffmpeg/ffprobe/yt-dlp
subprocess calls used for transcription and audio processing.

Add backend/run.py that sets WindowsProactorEventLoopPolicy before
uvicorn starts, and disables --reload on Windows (the root cause).
Update SETUP_GUIDE.md Windows commands to use 'python run.py'.

Closes #1

Co-authored-by: Yuval Avidani <info@yuv.ai>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: AdLib BPM/speed computation and DMO instrument extraction

- Compute BPM from OPL refresh rate instead of using placeholder 125
- For capture formats: derive speed + BPM from refresh rate with
  compensation for very slow (<20Hz) and very fast (>200Hz) formats
- For native formats: compute BPM from player refresh when not reported
- Clamp BPM to 32-300 range, adjusting speed to compensate
- Fix S3M/DMO instrument extraction: allow OPL types 2+3, not just 0
- Add adplug_get_refresh_rate() API for all player types
- All 36 formats now have valid BPM, speed, and instrument data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve yt-dlp/ffmpeg/ffprobe paths from venv on Windows (#9)

On Windows, when yt-dlp, ffmpeg, and ffprobe are installed only inside a
virtual environment, their executables are not on the system PATH. This
caused FileNotFoundError in asyncio.create_subprocess_exec calls.

Add exec_resolver.py that checks the venv Scripts/bin directory first,
then falls back to shutil.which() and the bare command name. Replace all
hardcoded command strings in url_downloader.py and audio_processor.py
with resolved constants.

Fixes #2

Co-authored-by: Yuval Avidani <info@yuv.ai>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

O _isCoLeader retornava false quando o membro nao tinha userId
(sem conta de login), impedindo o botao 'Remover como Co-lider'
de aparecer para qualquer role (lider, supervisor, pastor, admin).

Agora: se isLeader=true e nao eh o leaderId principal da celula,
eh co-lider independente de ter userId ou nao.

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): add --process-type option to override process execution mode (#183)

# Summary

Adds `--process-type` CLI option to `plugboard process run` for
switching between `LocalProcess` and `RayProcess` execution modes,
overriding YAML configuration. Uses `Literal` type for type-safe
validation.

# Changes

- **CLI Enhancement**
- Added `--process-type {local|ray}` option to `run` command with
`Literal["local", "ray"]` type
- Typer automatically validates input and shows helpful error messages
for invalid values

- **Schema Enhancement**
- Added `RAY_CONNECTOR_CLS_PATH` constant to connector schema for
consistency
- Implemented `ProcessSpec.override_process_type()` method with
mapping-based logic
- Method handles process type, connector builder, and state backend
updates atomically
- When switching to `ray`: `LocalProcess` → `RayProcess`,
`AsyncioConnector` → `RayConnector`, `DictStateBackend` →
`RayStateBackend`
  - When switching to `local`: reverses the above transformations
  - Preserves connector builder args during override
- Only modifies incompatible components (e.g., won't change
`RayConnector` when already on Ray)

- **Tests**
  - Unit tests for local override and ray override
  - Validation testing handled by typer's built-in Literal type support

**Example usage:**
```bash
# Override any YAML config to run locally
plugboard process run ray-config.yaml --process-type local

# Force Ray execution regardless of YAML
plugboard process run local-config.yaml --process-type ray
```

**Implementation Note:** The override logic is implemented in the schema
layer (`ProcessSpec.override_process_type()`) using a mapping-based
approach rather than hard-coded if/else branches in the CLI, keeping
business logic close to the data model and improving maintainability.
Type safety is enforced using `Literal` types, allowing typer to handle
validation automatically.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>CLI option to switch between local and Ray
process</issue_title>
> <issue_description>See
https://github.com/plugboard-dev/plugboard/pull/49#discussion_r1869413176
> 
> We would like to be able to switch between Local and Ray processes
from the CLI.</issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>We need an option on the CLI to switch between
`RayProcess` and `LocalProcess` by overriding whatever is specified in
the input YAML. Note that when changing from local to ray, it is not
enough just to change the `Process` type: you will also need to make
sure the `connector_builder` and `State` type are changed if they are
specified in the YAML and not compatible with Ray.</agent_instructions>

  Try/catch per step, -WhatIf/-Verbose support, 30-day log pruning

scripts/backup.ps1:
  Generic local backup to ~/wiki-recall-backup/YYYY-MM-DD/
  Excludes rebuildable dirs, keeps last 7 backups

scripts/setup-scheduler.ps1:
  Registers 3 Windows Task Scheduler tasks:
  - Hourly maintenance (harvest+backup+lint+compact)
  - Hourly backup (offset 30 min)
  - Nightly full run (wakes machine)
  Supports -Uninstall to remove all tasks

scripts/setup-cron.sh:
  Cross-platform cron for Mac/Linux (hourly/daily, --uninstall)

Updated setup.ps1 with maintenance wizard (Step 11)

1,432 tests, 0 failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: support <data> (variable-length fields) inside groups (#91) (#107)

Parse <data> elements within groups, store in SchemaGroupDto, and
generate group-level data callbacks in ConsumeVariableLengthSegments.

Changes:
- SchemaGroupDto: add optional Data list (List<SchemaDataDto>)
- SchemaReader.ReadGroup(): parse <data> child elements
- GroupDefinition: add Datas property with TypedDatas accessor
- MessagesCodeGenerator.BuildGroups(): pass group data through
- MessageDefinition: include group data callbacks in params/args,
  read group data after each entry with reader.TrySkip advancement

Fix open Dependabot alerts in website dependencies (#1681)

Update vulnerable npm packages to patched versions:
- lodash-es: 4.17.21 → 4.18.1 (fixes Prototype Pollution and Code Injection)
- path-to-regexp: 0.1.12 → 0.1.13 (fixes ReDoS)
- serialize-javascript: 6.0.2 → 7.0.5 (fixes RCE and CPU exhaustion DoS)
- minimatch: 3.1.2 → 3.1.5 (fixes ReDoS)

Added yarn resolutions to force serialize-javascript and lodash-es
to patched versions across all transitive dependencies.
Updated overrides to match current patched versions.
